title: Neonauclea bernardoi
intro: Neonauclea bernardoi, also known as the "Malayan blue mahang," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neonauclea bernardoi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neonauclea bernardoi is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas and capsules for its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Neonauclea bernardoi is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Philippines. The classification of Neonauclea bernardoi places it within the genus Neonauclea, which is a group of plants commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as the madder or coffee family. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Neonauclea bernardoi.

The plant is reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its use in treating wounds and infections.
